steps.title: Enter approximate points from the scatterplot
steps.content: The data rise faster and faster, so use an exponential regression in Desmos. Approximate the plotted points and enter them as two lists.

steps.title: Run exponential regression
steps.content: Type $y_1\sim ab^{x_1}$. The fitted curve is increasing and exponential, which eliminates the straight-line choices. The fitted growth also has the same general scale as choice D, not the curve that sits above every point.

steps.title: Graph-check choice D
steps.content: Graph choice D and compare it to the plotted points. It follows the same accelerating shape and has points on both sides of the curve, while choice C is systematically too high. That makes choice D the best model.
